allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking gives second-order-thinking the structure needed to trace how local moves create later effects through interaction and emergence.
allies.source_quote: Systems Thinking: As the primary ally, Systems Thinking provides the necessary structure to map out the consequences inherent in SOT.

allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops help second-order-thinking inspect whether a locally helpful move will reinforce or destabilize the broader system over time.
allies.source_quote: The plan is approved because the immediate gain is visible, while the real outcome is created later by overloaded dependencies, delayed reactions, or local teams protecting their own metrics.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias makes second-order-thinking tell a downstream story that protects the preferred plan instead of stress-testing it.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Second-order-thinking conflicts with bottlenecks when a local acceleration hides the downstream threshold where delay or overload flips the result into a worse outcome.
structured_tensions.source_quote: identify the first threshold where delay, overload, or information-hiding would cascade into a worse second-order outcome

structured_tensions.tension_text: Second-order-thinking conflicts with incentives when local teams protect their own metrics in ways that make the broader system worse.
structured_tensions.source_quote: name the incentive that would cause a local team to protect its metric while making the system worse

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on downstream system mapping and incentive-aware stress tests, so the file keeps a compact normalized relation surface instead of inventing speculative chain catalogs.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor-style multihop logic or long consequence ladders because this batch stops at first-order relation semantics.
open_questions: Availability and hammer effects remain source-real antagonists, but this conservative pass kept the strongest curated-system linkages.
